name: Poster-Tile Mosaic · 3 beat-synced operations
one_line: ONE base template — a packed MOSAIC of DIFFERENT-sized colored tiles (asset-free: color + label) that tessellate to fill the frame with no overlap — driven by three interchangeable beat-synced operations: staggered enter/exit, locked global-recolor, and snake-fill + overlay.

base_template.notes: A packed mosaic of DIFFERENT-sized absolutely-positioned tiles (NOT a uniform grid): tall / wide / small boxes that tessellate to fill the frame with no overlap (thin seams only) — the source's 错落 wall. Built by a deterministic recursive binary-split packer (split the largest region along its longer side; ratio is a fixed function of the step index). A tile = a colored box + a text label (stands in for an image; no assets). 'Add a box' = a new image; 'change a box color' = a new asset / recolor. All three operations below act on this one mosaic — that is the whole point: same tiles, different rhythmic scheduling.

A_staggerInOut.intent: build the mosaic by popping DIFFERENT-sized tiles in one-per-onset (top-right→bottom-left waterfall) until they pack the frame with no overlap, hold on a downbeat, then pop them out one-per-onset
A_staggerInOut.anchors: enter[] = onsets; hold = downbeat; exit[] = onsets
A_staggerInOut.what_moves: transform + opacity per tile (scatter-jitter slide-in, drop-out); tiles vary in size and tessellate — they do NOT overlap

B_holdRecolor.intent: snap the whole wall on at once, LOCK positions, recolor the entire grid as one unit on each beat
B_holdRecolor.anchors: on = onset; recolor[] = snares (one palette swap each); off = onset
B_holdRecolor.what_moves: background color of every tile (0ms set — a recolor is a hit, not a fade). Positions never change.

C_snakeFillOverlay.intent: reveal tiles along a snake path to fill the grid, recolor the whole grid on the hard-stop, then pop overlay 'cutouts' on top one-per-onset
C_snakeFillOverlay.anchors: fill[] = onsets (incl. a downbeat accel); swap = hard_stop; overlay[] = onsets
C_snakeFillOverlay.snake_path: top row L→R, then bottom row R→L, repeating per row pair
C_snakeFillOverlay.what_moves: per-tile reveal (opacity+scale) along the path; one full-grid palette swap on the hard-stop; overlay elements pop in and HOLD (no exit)

match.notes: This is a META-template: a wall + a pluggable list of operations. Reach for it when a section has many discrete, individually-placeable onsets (a hit for every tile move) AND distinct sub-phases you want to articulate differently — accumulate, hold-and-flash, then fill-and-stack. The three ops map naturally onto: build → recolor-on-roll → fill-then-drop.

tiles.notes: number of boxes the packer tessellates the frame into

bands.notes: horizontal row-bands the snake fill (OP C) sweeps through

program.notes: JSON array of operator steps (see program_format). Empty = built-in group_5 score. THIS is the compose-by-data surface: reorder/drop/repeat steps, no code edit.

engine.model: operators + program-as-data. The 3 operators (§6 of index.html) are pure, parameterized, order-independent functions over ONE shared mosaic. A PROGRAM (data) lists which run, in what order, on which beats; §7 dispatches it. Compose by editing DATA (the PROGRAM array, the `program` variable, or program.json) — never the operator code. Steps combine freely, repeat, and run solo.
engine.dispatch: OPS[step.op](step) for each step in PROGRAM
engine.extend: add an operator = add a function + an OPS key; the program can then call it by name

program_format._: A program is a JSON array of steps. Each step = { op, ...fields }. Beats are absolute audio seconds. Themes are indices into THEMES[]. order = 'waterfall' | 'snake' | 'index' | [explicit tile-index array].

staggerInOut.enter: number[]  — onset per tile (distributed if count != tiles); reveal in `order`
staggerInOut.exit: number[]? — onset per tile to drop out; omit to leave the wall standing
staggerInOut.hold: number?   — documentary downbeat the full wall lands on (no tween)
staggerInOut.theme: number?   — THEMES index dressed at reveal (default 0)

holdRecolor.on: number   — all tiles snap on at once here
holdRecolor.recolor: number[]? — beats at which the WHOLE wall recolors as a unit
holdRecolor.themes: number[]? — THEMES index applied at each recolor beat (cycled)
holdRecolor.off: number?  — clear the wall here; omit to hold it

snakeFillOverlay.fill: number[] — reveal anchors; tiles distributed across them in `order`
snakeFillOverlay.baseTheme: number?  — THEMES index for the fill (default 0)
snakeFillOverlay.swap: number?  — beat for a full-grid recolor (the hard-stop 一键换装)
snakeFillOverlay.swapTheme: number?  — THEMES index for the swap (default 4)
snakeFillOverlay.overlay: number[]? — one cutout pops per beat, holds to the end

program_data: program.json (the default group_5 score as standalone, agent-editable data)

provenance.video_read: group_5.gemini-analysis.md (Gemini 3 Pro) corrected by frame-by-frame inspection — Gemini mislabeled op B as a zero-gap banner and called the three a 'different systems'; pulled frames proved B is the same tile-wall recolored as a unit, and all three share one asset family / grid paradigm.
